questions.text: HCl is added to the following oxides. Which one would give H2O2?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is not a peroxide.
This is not a peroxide.
This is not a peroxide.

MnO2, PbO2 and BaO will not give H2O2 with HCl. MnO2 and PbO2 will give H2O2 with Cl2 and BaO will react with HCl to give BaCl2 and water. Since none of the oxides is a peroxide. Hence none of them would give H2O2 as only peroxides give H2O2.

questions.explanation: MnO2, PbO2 and BaO will not give H2O2 with HCl. MnO2 and PbO2 will give H2O2 with Cl2 and BaO will react with HCl to give BaCl2 and water. Since none of the oxides is a peroxide. Hence none of them would give H2O2 as only peroxides give H2O2.

questions.text: Which is the strongest base?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: NaOH is the strongest base since it is known to dissociate completely, to form Sodium (Na+) ions and Hydroxide (OH-) ions. Moreover, to substantiate further, NaOH reacts completely with acid, in a neutralization reaction.
Mg(OH)2 is less stronger base than NaOH.
This is a weak base.
NaOH is a strong base.

questions.explanation: NaOH is the strongest base since it is known to dissociate completely, to form Sodium (Na+) ions and Hydroxide (OH-) ions. Moreover, to substantiate further, NaOH reacts completely with acid, in a neutralization reaction.

questions.text: An example of a covalent hydride is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is an ionic hydride.
This is ionic hydride.
This is ionic hydride.

Arsine (AsH3) is a covalent hydride. It is a compound in which arsenic (As) is bonded to three hydrogen (H) atoms through covalent bonds. Covalent hydrides are compounds in which hydrogen is covalently bonded to nonmetals or metalloids, and in the case of arsine, it is bonded to arsenic. Arsine is a colorless, highly toxic, and flammable gas with a garlic-like odor. It is used in certain industrial applications but must be handled with extreme care due to its toxicity.

questions.explanation: Arsine (AsH3) is a covalent hydride. It is a compound in which arsenic (As) is bonded to three hydrogen (H) atoms through covalent bonds. Covalent hydrides are compounds in which hydrogen is covalently bonded to nonmetals or metalloids, and in the case of arsine, it is bonded to arsenic. Arsine is a colorless, highly toxic, and flammable gas with a garlic-like odor. It is used in certain industrial applications but must be handled with extreme care due to its toxicity.

questions.text: H2O2 cannot be synthesized by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Addition of ice cold H2SO4 on BaO2
options.explanation: H2O2 can be prepared by addition of ice cold H2SO4 on BaO2

options.text: Addition of ice cold H2SO4 on PbO2
options.explanation: Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) cannot be prepared by adding ice-cold sulfuric acid (H2SO4) to lead dioxide (PbO2). This is because the reaction of PbO2 with H2SO4 does not produce hydrogen peroxide. Instead, the reaction typically results in the formation of lead sulfate (PbSO4) and water (H2O): PbO2 + H2SO4 → PbSO4 + H2O In this reaction, the lead dioxide is reacting with sulfuric acid to produce lead sulfate and water, not hydrogen peroxide.
H2O2 can be prepared by aerial oxidation of 2 ethyl anthraquinol

options.text: Electrolysis of (NH4)2SO4 at a high current density
options.explanation: It can be prepared by electrolysis of (NH4)2SO4 at a high current density

questions.explanation: Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) cannot be prepared by adding ice-cold sulfuric acid (H2SO4) to lead dioxide (PbO2). This is because the reaction of PbO2 with H2SO4 does not produce hydrogen peroxide. Instead, the reaction typically results in the formation of lead sulfate (PbSO4) and water (H2O): PbO2 + H2SO4 → PbSO4 + H2O In this reaction, the lead dioxide is reacting with sulfuric acid to produce lead sulfate and water, not hydrogen peroxide.

questions.text: Which one of the following processes will produce hard water?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Permanent hardness of water is due to chlorides and sulphates of calcium and magnesium i.e. CaCl2, CaSO4, MgCl2 and MgSO4.

Permanent hardness of water is due to chlorides and sulphates of calcium and magnesium i.e. CaCl2, CaSO4, MgCl2 and MgSO4. Saturation of water with CaSO4 can lead to the formation of calcium ions and sulfate ions (SO₄²⁻). It can contribute to the overall mineral content of the water and is more commonly associated with the concept of "total hardness."
Addition of Na2SO4 (sodium sulfate) to water does not produce hard water. Sodium sulfate does not introduce calcium or magnesium ions, which are the primary contributors to water hardness.
Permanent hardness of water is due to chlorides and sulphates of calcium and magnesium i.e. CaCl2, CaSO4, MgCl2 and MgSO4.

questions.explanation: Permanent hardness of water is due to chlorides and sulphates of calcium and magnesium i.e. CaCl2, CaSO4, MgCl2 and MgSO4. Saturation of water with CaSO4 can lead to the formation of calcium ions and sulfate ions (SO₄²⁻) but is not typically associated with temporary hardness. It can contribute to the overall mineral content of the water but is more commonly associated with the concept of "total hardness."

questions.text: 4D2O + 3Fe → Fe3O4 + gas. The gas produced in the above reaction is:-
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The gas produced in this reaction is deuterium gas (2D2)
The gas produced in this reaction is deuterium gas (2D2)

The reaction is the reaction between heavy water (deuterium oxide, 4D2O) and iron (Fe) to produce a compound called iron(III) oxide (Fe3O4) and gas. The gas produced in this reaction is deuterium gas (2D2), which is a form of hydrogen gas composed of deuterium atoms (D) instead of normal hydrogen atoms (H). Deuterium is a heavy isotope of hydrogen and is used in various scientific and industrial applications, including nuclear reactors and nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) spectroscopy.
The gas produced in this reaction is deuterium gas (2D2)

questions.text: Which of the following compound affects mercury:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Please refer to the main explanation.
Please refer to the main explanation.

Ozone (O3) can react with mercury (Hg) and affect it in various ways, particularly in environmental contexts. Ozone is known to oxidize elemental mercury (Hg) and convert it into mercury compounds, such as mercury(II) oxide (HgO) and mercuric chloride (HgCl2), which are less volatile and more likely to be removed from the atmosphere. This process can contribute to the removal of mercury from the air and its eventual deposition to the Earth's surface, reducing the environmental impact of atmospheric mercury pollution. In certain atmospheric and environmental conditions, ozone plays a role in the transformation and cycling of mercury, which is an important consideration in the context of controlling and understanding mercury pollution.
Please refer to the main explanation.

questions.explanation: Ozone (O3) can react with mercury (Hg) and affect it in various ways, particularly in environmental contexts. Ozone is known to oxidize elemental mercury (Hg) and convert it into mercury compounds, such as mercury(II) oxide (HgO) and mercuric chloride (HgCl2), which are less volatile and more likely to be removed from the atmosphere. This process can contribute to the removal of mercury from the air and its eventual deposition to the Earth's surface, reducing the environmental impact of atmospheric mercury pollution. In certain atmospheric and environmental conditions, ozone plays a role in the transformation and cycling of mercury, which is an important consideration in the context of controlling and understanding mercury pollution.

questions.text: Which of the following is only acidic in nature?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Refer to the main explanation.
Refer to the main explanation.

Among the compounds listed, boric acid B(OH)3 is the only one that is exclusively acidic in nature. Boric acid does not have hydroxide ions (OH-) to exhibit basic properties, and it primarily acts as a Lewis acid, meaning it can accept a pair of electrons. It forms Lewis acid-base complexes and does not possess the characteristics of a hydroxide or base. The other compounds, such as beryllium hydroxide Be(OH)2, magnesium hydroxide Mg(OH)2, and aluminum hydroxide Al(OH)3, all contain hydroxide ions (OH-) and are, therefore, basic in nature. They can readily accept protons (H+) and exhibit basic properties.
Refer to the main explanation.

questions.explanation: Among the compounds listed, boric acid B(OH)3 is the only one that is exclusively acidic in nature. It forms Lewis acid-base complexes and does not possess the characteristics of a hydroxide or base. The other compounds, such as beryllium hydroxide Be(OH)2, magnesium hydroxide Mg(OH)2, and aluminum hydroxide Al(OH)3, all contain hydroxide ions (OH-) and are, therefore, basic in nature. They can readily accept protons (H+) and exhibit basic properties.

questions.text: Alkaline earth metals produce bright blue colour when they are dissolved in liquid ammonia. It is due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: d-d transition in the solvated metal ion
options.explanation: The bright blue color produced when alkaline earth metals are dissolved in liquid ammonia is due to the presence of solvated electrons. These are electrons that are surrounded by a shell of solvent molecules, which in this case is the liquid ammonia. The solvated electrons are responsible for the blue color because they absorb light in the visible region of the spectrum and emit light in the blue region. The blue color is also an indication that the metal has lost its valence electrons, which have been solvated by the ammonia. This is because metals in the alkaline earth group have two valence electrons, which are easily lost to form a 2+ ion.

The blue color is also an indication that the metal has lost its valence electrons, which have been solvated by the ammonia. This is because metals in the alkaline earth group have two valence electrons, which are easily lost to form a 2+ ion.

options.text: absorption of light by the solvated electrons.

questions.explanation: Like alkali metals, alkaline earth metals also dissolve in liquid ammonia to form coloured solutions. Dilute solutions are bright blue in colour due to solvated electrons. The ammoniated electrons absorb energy corresponding to a red region of visible light. Therefore, the transmitted light is blue in colour. M + (x + 2y) NH3 → [M(NH3)x]2+ + 2e−(NH3)y (blue)

questions.text: Which of the following statement is incorrect when a mixture of NaCl and K2Cr2O7 is strongly heated with conc. H2SO4?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Deep red orange coloured vapours are evolved.
options.explanation: The mixture of NaCl and K2Cr2O7 when strongly heated with conc. H2SO4 will produce chlorine gas (Cl2) and chromium trioxide (CrO3), which is a powerful oxidizing agent. Therefore, the incorrect statement in this case could be that the mixture will produce a reducing agent. This is not true, as the chromium trioxide produced is a strong oxidizing agent. The correct statement would be that the mixture will produce Cl2 gas and CrO3, which are both colored compounds. The Cl2 gas will be identified by its yellow-green color and the CrO3 will be identified by its orange-red color.

questions.explanation: This is the following solution:

questions.text: The paramagnetic species is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: KO2 (potassium superoxide) is paramagnetic. This is because it has an unpaired electron in its molecular orbital configuration. The presence of an unpaired electron makes the compound paramagnetic, which means that it is attracted to a magnetic field. Potassium superoxide is a dark yellow or orange solid that is used as a source of oxygen in chemical reactions. Its paramagnetic nature is due to the presence of the superoxide ion (O2-) in its structure, which has an unpaired electron.

SiO2 (silicon dioxide), also known as silica, is actually considered to be diamagnetic. This means that it is not attracted to a magnetic field and has no unpaired electrons in its molecular orbital configuration. The reason for this is that the electrons in the outermost energy level of silicon and oxygen atoms are involved in forming covalent bonds with each other, resulting in a stable electronic configuration and no unpaired electrons.

TiO2 (titanium dioxide) is diamagnetic in nature. This means that it is not attracted to a magnetic field and has no unpaired electrons in its molecular orbital configuration. The reason for this is that the electrons in the outermost energy level of titanium and oxygen atoms are involved in forming covalent bonds with each other, resulting in a stable electronic configuration and no unpaired electrons. Therefore, TiO2 has no magnetic moment and is not attracted to a magnetic field.

BaO2 (barium peroxide) is actually diamagnetic in nature. This is because it has no unpaired electrons in its molecular orbital configuration. The electrons in the outermost energy level of barium and oxygen atoms are involved in forming covalent bonds with each other, resulting in a stable electronic configuration and no unpaired electrons. Therefore, BaO2 is not attracted to a magnetic field and has no magnetic moment.

questions.text: Choose the incorrect statement.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: BeCO3 is preserved in an atmosphere of CO2 as it is thermally least stable.
options.explanation: BeCO3​ has lowest thermal stability. It decomposes to give BeO and CO2​. Hence, it is placed in the atmosphere of CO2​ which prevents decomposition.BeCO3→BeO+ CO2

options.text: BeF2 forms a complex compound with excess NaF, in which the complex entity containing Be, is a cation.
options.explanation: Beryllium fluoride (BeF2) forms a complex compound with excess sodium fluoride (NaF), in which the complex entity containing Be is an anion. The complex is called sodium tetrafluoroberyllate (Na2[BeF4]2-). The formation of the complex can be represented by the following equation: BeF2 + 2NaF → Na2[BeF4]2- In the complex, the beryllium ion (Be2+) is surrounded by four fluoride ions (F-). The beryllium ion has a charge of +2, while each fluoride ion has a charge of -1. The overall charge of the complex is -2. The complex is an anion because it has a negative charge. The negative charge is due to the presence of the four fluoride ions. The fluoride ions are attracted to the beryllium ion because of the opposite charges.

options.text: Beryllium dissolves in an alkali to form [Be(OH)4]2-
options.explanation: Beryllium dissolves in alkali to form [Be(OH)4]-2.Be+2 + 4OH- → [Be(OH)4]-2

options.text: Beryllium exhibits no diagonal relationship with sodium.
options.explanation: Beryllium shows a diagonal relationship with Aluminium. They both have nearly equal electronegativity, small size and availability of only four orbitals in the valence shell. They both form amphoteric oxides and dimeric halides.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.Beryllium fluoride (BeF2) forms a complex compound with excess sodium fluoride (NaF), in which the complex entity containing Be is an anion. The complex is called sodium tetrafluoroberyllate (Na2[BeF4]2-). The formation of the complex can be represented by the following equation: BeF2 + 2NaF → Na2[BeF4]2- In the complex, the beryllium ion (Be2+) is surrounded by four fluoride ions (F-). The beryllium ion has a charge of +2, while each fluoride ion has a charge of -1. The overall charge of the complex is -2. The complex is an anion because it has a negative charge. The negative charge is due to the presence of the four fluoride ions. The fluoride ions are attracted to the beryllium ion because of the opposite charges.

questions.text: Cryolite is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Calcium fluoride is a compound with the chemical formula CaF2. It is commonly used in the production of optical lenses and windows due to its transparency to ultraviolet and infrared light. It is also added to toothpaste and water supplies to help prevent tooth decay.

Potassium hexafluoroaluminate, also known as cryolite, is a compound with the chemical formula K3AlF6. It is commonly used in the production of aluminum as a flux to lower the melting point of the alumina. This helps in reducing energy consumption during the aluminum smelting process.
Basic magnesium carbonate, also known as magnesite, is a compound that is commonly used as a mineral supplement and in the production of refractory materials. It has a chemical formula of MgCO3.
Cryolite is an ore of sodium and has the formula Na3[AlF6]. Therefore IUPAC name of this complex salt is sodium hexafluoroaluminate(III).

questions.explanation: Cryolite is an ore of sodium and has the formula Na3[AlF6]. Therefore IUPAC name of this complex salt is sodium hexafluoroaluminate(III).
